theme: water valves bound the damage of any burst
title: Valve Segmentation Under a Double-Burst Sweep
seed_problem: min-max balanced graph partitioning under a cut-edge budget
mutation: composite: goal becomes minimizing the worst segment demand over an enumerated burst sweep (single pipe bursts, exhaustively) AND a curated double-burst list whose pairwise segment-union loss can dominate the single-burst max, so the intended solution is a demand-balanced tree segmentation that pre-empts which valve-eligible cuts would separate a listed pair.
strategies: trivial: install no valves at all (the checker's own do-nothing reference, ratio 0.1)

naive greedy: sort pipes by their OWN demand descending and spend the valve budget fencing off whichever junction hub feeds the single biggest pipe, ignoring total segment demand and the double-burst list entirely

insight (strong): recover the valve-eligible contraction tree, pre-glue every double-burst pair's two segments by forbidding cuts on the tree path between them, then binary-search the smallest max-segment achievable by removing at most V of the remaining cut sites (a demand-balanced tree partition) -- this both avoids the flagship-pipe trap and anticipates which pairs would otherwise union
further headroom: exact/ILP tree partitioning with double-burst coupling, or local search that trades a slightly worse single-segment balance for fewer forced double-burst unions
